column65:
I want to preface this by saying everything about the clan has been improving over the years and this is the best version of EG we have ever had. 

That said, changes are incredibly slow and incremental, in part because it all seems reliant on one or two people doing everything. There are sections of the clan, like mentoring and other games, that feel abandoned and lost. Despite a punishment system, some people get instantly kicked for things others say without any discipline. I think the burnout of some staff members is apparent and there doesn't seem to be proper support for them or a backup plan. It is unclear what certain leaders actually do to keep things running smoothly and it seems grossly unfair for Lexi to consistently have to pull a disproportionate amount of weight. 

In other industries there is this idea of 'How would your project/company run if someone critical got hit by a bus?' If something happened and forced some people to step down, I'm not confident the clan would be ok.

I think this could be a hot take, but the feeling I get is that people are too comfortable within their own friends groups within EG. My few points to rise on this are the increasing amount of people asking to be matched with XYZ during the bigger events  (e.g GR) and the lack of interaction with the #team-search channel. This is obviously anecdotical, but I've made most of my connections through events and the events really do feel different when you're amongst a group of friends who don't communicate much with the team's chat/toss invite and suggestion to others and all that. 

As a day-to-day, the social atmosphere of the Discord seems pretty active with the regular voicers and chatters, but as someone who's not part of these group, it's pretty hard to find a place to fit in if you're not a loud voice as there's a lot of inside jokes/banters or simply just a lot of people at once in a VC! Might just be a me skill issue but that do be how I feel.

Albeit hectic and crazy at time in the channels or in-game chat, EG is still very well organized and know where to draw the line between fun and seriousness.

I'd love to see more recurrent "Group-Up" flash event where clanmates are encouraged to come together and do things. I could be led by mentors for lesser experienced members and be done in smaller groups rather than masses we've seen. E.g I'd love to see these events encourage people to run trios TOA rather than form an 8-man party as to differentiate the "group-up" aspect vs Mass and maybe encourage less experienced players to have a real taste of the content and come learn, smaller CoX teams rather than masses to better maybe even a scaled raid learning for the higher gamers I dunno! It doesn't have to be raids either, could be a monthly small team nex/gwd where it's trying to make small groups and there's mentors for the less experienced players to group up with... 

I'd basically just love to see more content aimed towards inclusiveness, trying to form smaller groups and maybe push less experienced players towards new content and also encourage the less active/more shy players to join smaller groups.

I'd love to see more visibility on what the clan leadership is dealing with so we feel more invested and included in the whole processes as well as better see the meaning behind the roles. 

Hope it helps love y'all
